messages.content: hello
Hello! How can I assist you today?
Please ask me competency based interview questions for a supply planner role in loreal

Sure, here are some competency-based interview questions for a supply planner role in L'Oreal:

1. Can you describe your experience in managing and analyzing data to optimize inventory levels?
2. Can you give an example of a time when you had to work cross-functionally with other departments to ensure on-time product delivery to customers?
3. How do you prioritize conflicting demands on your time when dealing with production and logistics requirements?
4. Tell me about a time when you had to proactively identify and mitigate risks to supply chain performance.
5. Can you describe a time when you had to troubleshoot a supply chain issue quickly and effectively to minimize disruptions to production?
6. Describe a project that required you to learn a new skill or process quickly, and how you approached it.
7. Tell me about a time when you identified an opportunity for cost savings or process improvement within the supply chain, and how you implemented it successfully.
8. Can you describe your experience in analyzing demand and supply data to develop long-term production and capacity plans?
9. Tell me about a time when you successfully navigated a challenging interpersonal situation with a colleague or stakeholder.
10. Can you give an example of a time when you leveraged technology or automation to improve supply chain efficiencies?
please ask one at a time and tthen rate my answers

Sure, I will ask one question at a time. Go ahead and answer the first question:

1. Can you describe your experience in managing and analyzing data to optimize inventory levels?
I find that a data based approach is the most ideal and easy to get behind when it comes to setting inventory levels and balancing priorities
Thank you for your response. Can you please provide a more detailed example of how you have managed and analyzed data to optimize inventory levels in a previous role?

During my current role, i would conduct regular analyzes of our portfolio to re-align our set inventory levels. This included analyzing our demand data (forecast accuracy and variance) as well as looking at supply replenishment elements such as lead time changes and site capacity

Thank you for sharing that. Could you also explain how you use this information to optimize inventory levels and what strategies you've used in the past to ensure they are balanced with other priorities such as cost management, customer service levels, and sales targets?

In terms of strategy i would check our current performance and identify the root causes for the biggest offenders in terms of OOS and overstock. based on this i would work to re-align our Inventory targets. I would also take into account the mix of high cost items and commercial priorities

Great, thank you for your response. Based on what you've shared, it seems like you take a thoughtful and analytical approach to inventory management, incorporating data analysis and a consideration of various factors that affect inventory levels. 

I would give your answer a rating of 4 out of 5, as it was clear and provided some specific information about how you have managed and analyzed data to optimize inventory levels. To improve your answer, you could consider providing more specific examples or metrics of how your approach has improved inventory performance or demonstrate how you have balanced inventory priorities in the past.
Give me an example of an ideal answer

Sure, here's an example of an ideal answer for this question:

"In my previous role as a supply planner at XYZ cosmetics company, I was responsible for optimizing inventory levels across multiple product lines. To achieve this, I regularly analyzed demand and supply data, including sales forecasts, lead times, and order frequency. I also worked closely with cross-functional teams, including production, logistics, and sales, to ensure that we had the right level of inventory to support customer demand and meet company sales targets.

One specific example of how I used data to optimize inventory levels was when we had a high-performing product line where we were consistently running out of stock. Upon analysis, I identified that our forecasts for this product line had been consistently low, causing us to under-stock. To solve the issue, I worked to refine our forecasting model for that product line, incorporating additional data sources and adjusting parameters accordingly. As a result, we were able to meet demand more accurately and maintain a healthy level of inventory, while also avoiding excess stock.

To ensure that inventory optimization was balanced with other priorities such as cost management and sales targets, I regularly reviewed our performance against key metrics such as inventory turnover and customer service levels. I also worked to improve communication and collaboration with our sales team, ensuring that we had visibility into promotional activities and new product launches, which allowed us to better anticipate demand and adjust inventory levels accordingly. Overall, I believe that my data-driven approach, coupled with my ability to work collaboratively across functions, allowed me to successfully optimize inventory levels and support business goals."
great, next question

Sure, here is the next question:

2. Can you give an example of a time when you had to work cross-functionally with other departments to ensure on-time product delivery to customers?

sure, for new products i work in tandem with commercial functions to understand what their aspirations are and align the supply plan accordingly in addition i have regular meeting with them to get insight on any upcoming spike on demand and sales in order to react accordingly and proactively

Thanks for sharing that. Could you provide more details about a specific instance where you worked with a commercial team to ensure on-time delivery of a product, and what actions you took to ensure success?

For a new product launch i did the following:
I checked the expected annual forecasts and aligned it with the site to ensure available capacity for the sku
I would also clearly communicate the lead times and expected site agility in terms of replenishment
I would meet with the stakeholders regularly to stay up to date on any delays that impact the production date
I would communicate first shipment dispatch to ensure logistics are ready for Clearance and Inbound

Thank you for providing more details on how you worked with a commercial team to ensure on-time delivery for a new product launch. It appears that you took a collaborative approach which involved aligning demand forecasts with site capacity, communicating lead times and production timeframes, and meeting with stakeholders regularly to ensure everyone is on the same page.

Based on your response, I would give your answer a rating of 3.5 out of 5. While your answer shows that you have experience in working cross-functionally to ensure timely delivery of products, you could consider providing more specific examples or metrics of how your approach has improved on-time delivery of products and exceeded customer expectations. This can help to demonstrate your impact in this area.

Would you like the next question?
Give me an ideal answer

Sure, here is an example of an ideal answer:

"In my previous role as a supply planner for a global beauty company, I worked closely with the commercial team to ensure on-time delivery of new product launches. One example of this was when we were launching a new skincare line that was expected to generate high demand. To ensure successful product launch, I worked collaboratively with the commercial team to understand their goals and priorities, including their expected sales forecasts and launch timelines.

Based on this input, I conducted a thorough analysis of our production capacity and raw material availability, ensuring we had enough production slots and material supply to support the forecasted demand. I also worked closely with our procurement team to expedite any looming material shortages, creating alternative sources and developing contingency plans for producers not able to meet the lead times.

To keep all stakeholders informed and on track, I held weekly meetings with the commercial team, updating them on any production delays and ensuring they knew the status of each product, lead time targets, and expected ship dates. By staying proactive and working collaboratively with the commercial team and other functions, we were able to deliver all products surpassing the customer requested delivery date.

As a result of our efforts, we exceeded the customer expectations and generated record sales in the first month of launch. Overall, I believe that my collaborative approach to working cross-functionally, my attention to detail, and my ability to stay proactive and keep all stakeholders informed are some of my greatest strengths in ensuring the on-time delivery of products."
great next question

Here is the next question for you:

3. How do you prioritize conflicting demands on your time when dealing with production and logistics requirements?
